Okay, next funnel here is a tripwire funnel. This is one that Perry and Ryan have made famous over the last six months or so. And I think it's really cool the way that they do it. So this is, I think they have five steps in there, and this illustrates four of the five different things. So it was interesting about them that their business model is to kind of try to get everybody to fit into this mold, which I think is a good mold. I think it works for 90% of the businesses, but it is a funnel, one of many different types of funnel. So this is what they teach, and I think it's great. So the front end they have what they call a lead magnet, which is their squeeze page. They're giving away a free something, right? Boom. Then they, as soon as you get the free thing, they upsell you on what they call a tripwire, which is typically a super low ticket product. A lot of theirs are like $7 offers or free plus shipping. And from there they upsell the core offer, which is the actual product you want to sell, which is your middle value ladder type product. They downsell you on a payment plan for that product. And after that they try to profit maximize, which is puts you into their high ticket thing. Okay? I'm going to show you guys some of the funnels we use to get people in the high ticket thing here in a second, but that's kind of the way their funnel works. Okay? So I know that Ken's still in the room. Ken worked for Ryan and Perry for about six months or so, and this is what we did. Every single client that came in said, okay, boom, here's the five steps. What's your free plus shipping? Boom. What's your tripwire? And that's what he would do for them every single day with hundreds of clients. They go through that and build it out. This funnel works great. It's awesome. That's what that funnel looks like. What they do a lot of times is they'll do the same concept of this one thing, but they're like, what's the one thing? And they'll have you write like a 15 page report on that one thing, and they sell out for $7. And again, there's benefits to that. I told you guys early why. I don't like doing that. I like the word free. I want to use free as much as I can to get the right people in. But most of their offers in the internet marketing space, if you notice, are all $7 offers. All their products in the physical space are all free plus shipping. So it's the same concept, but it's your low end, front of the value ladder offer that you're bringing somebody through. One thing when they were first explained that was cool is that they said your core offers that most people are typically selling are. This is the core thing I'm selling. So what they did is they take your core thing and you just like take one piece out of it and make that the trip wire. And then same thing we talked about, the 100 visitor test, right? You get way more people buy your core offer because they already said yes to this very first thing. I agree 110% with everything they say on that. So that's kind of how they do theirs.
